Bullet appears in Notes; pill vanishes; call continues; jot-transcribe.com watermark lower-right

drafts.text:
Typing compresses. Speaking explains.

When you type a Slack reply: 'sounds good, will do' — 5 words.
When you speak the same thought: timeline, context, intent — 22 words.

Friction doesn't make you lazy. It compresses replies into non-answers.

I built Jot to fix this. Press a hotkey, speak, text at cursor — free, on-device Mac dictation.

Have you noticed your messages getting shorter over time?

#macOS #productivity #indiedev
drafts.note: Attach before-after-slack.svg (export as 1080×1080 PNG). Do NOT put the link in the post body — LinkedIn downranks posts with external links. Paste the utm_url as the FIRST COMMENT immediately after posting. Monday morning is the strongest LinkedIn window — post at 7am PT. Optional: post the 5-slide carousel as a LinkedIn document post for higher reach this week. Closing question is the engagement trigger.

drafts.text:
Typing: 'sounds good' — 5 words.
Speaking the same thought: 22 words of actual context.

Friction compresses replies into non-answers.

Jot: ⌥ Space → speak → text at cursor. Free. On-device.
drafts.note: Attach before-after-slack.svg (export as PNG). No link in post — post the utm_url as the first reply immediately after posting. ~159 characters, within the 200-char limit.

drafts.text:
The hidden reason your Slack messages are getting shorter (and what I did about it)

Have you noticed your replies getting less useful over time?

Mine did. I'd go to respond to a teammate's question and end up typing 'sounds good' instead of what I actually meant — not because I was lazy, but because typing mid-thought added just enough friction to compress everything into the shortest thing that technically answered the question.

I built Jot to fix this (disclosure: I made it). Press a hotkey anywhere on Mac, speak what you mean, and it pastes exactly where your cursor is — Slack, email, your code editor, anywhere. No window-switching, no separate step.

What I noticed after 30 days:
- My replies started including context ('since we moved to the new auth model...') instead of just answering the thing
- Threads got shorter — not because I wrote less, but because my first reply actually answered the thing
- I caught my own ambiguity mid-sentence, which you can't do when you're compressing to type fast

The less-obvious use case isn't writing documents. It's replacing all the small-text friction: the standup, the PR description, the Slack message where 'sounds good' is covering for 'I need 15 more seconds to explain what I mean.'

Jot is free, on-device on the Apple Neural Engine (no audio leaves your Mac), open source, Apple Silicon + macOS 15+.

(I built this — disclosure)

Link in comments.
drafts.note: Post to r/productivity. recommended:false — last recommended:true reddit was June 27 (2 days ago); needs 4+ days — earliest is July 1. Replace 'Link in comments' with the actual utm_url as the first comment. Answer every comment in the first hour.

Typing friction turns your full reply into 'sounds good.'

Hotkey → speak → 22 words of actual context. In any Mac app. Free. On-device.

Link in bio.

opportunities.title: Best Superwhisper Alternatives in 2026: Top Local AI Dictation Tools for Mac — BuildBetter
opportunities.why: New roundup not previously seen in search history. Targets users already open to local AI dictation — the exact Jot audience. Jot absent as the only fully-free open-source option. Check if comments are enabled.
opportunities.suggested_reply: One worth adding: Jot (jot-transcribe.com) — free, open-source, on-device on the Apple Neural Engine. Hotkey → speak → text at cursor in any Mac app. No subscription, no account, no audio leaves your Mac. 11 languages as of v1.16. I built it. (Disclosure: developer.)

opportunities.title: Wispr Flow Review 2026: Is It Worth $15/mo? — Spokenly
opportunities.why: Readers of a $15/month pricing review are the exact audience for a $0 alternative. Pricing frustration makes Jot's zero-cost model directly relevant. Check if comments are enabled.
opportunities.suggested_reply: If the $15/month is the sticking point, Jot is worth trying — free, open-source Mac dictation that transcribes entirely on-device on the Apple Neural Engine. No subscription, no account. I built it. (Disclosure: developer.) jot-transcribe.com
